title: OpenClaw v2026.5.18: Android Voice, Telegram Fixes & New Skills
description: OpenClaw v2026.5.18 ships real-time Android voice, Telegram topic routing fixes, new autoreview and meme-maker skills, Sharp image processing, and enterprise HTTPS proxy support for AI sales agents.
author: PulseAgent
content:
# OpenClaw v2026.5.18: Android Voice, Telegram Fixes & New Skills

OpenClaw v2026.5.18 dropped on May 18, 2026 — one of the more substantive releases in the 2026.5.x cycle. The headline improvements: **real-time voice calls on Android**, a targeted batch of **Telegram routing and reliability fixes**, an expanded **skills library**, and infrastructure improvements covering image processing, browser automation, and enterprise security. Here's what changes for B2B sales teams running AI SDR workflows on PulseAgent.

---

## Android Talk Mode Gets Real-Time Voice

The biggest UX improvement in this release: Talk Mode on Android now routes through the **realtime Gateway relay voice session**. Previously, voice interactions on mobile used a lower-quality fallback pathway. Starting v2026.5.18, Android clients get the same streaming mic input and realtime audio playback that desktop users have had for several releases.

For B2B export teams with field reps or account managers traveling cross-timezone, this means your AI SDR can handle **voice follow-up calls from any Android device** with the same quality as a desktop session. Whether your rep is at a trade show in Guangzhou or on the road between client meetings, voice sessions now work reliably.

> **Impact:** High for any team using PulseAgent's voice features for follow-up outreach or inbound qualification.

---

## Four Telegram Fixes That Matter for Sales Pipelines

Telegram is the dominant B2B outreach channel across Southeast Asia, Eastern Europe, the Middle East, and Latin America. This release ships four targeted improvements:

### Topic ID preserved across agent handoffs

In Telegram groups using forum topics, the `topic_id` was previously dropped when a lead transitioned between agents — for example, from a qualification bot to a closer. v2026.5.18 preserves topic context end-to-end, keeping all conversations correctly threaded in your group sales channels. No more replies landing in the general topic when they should be in a named deal thread.

### `/btw` and read-only commands no longer abort active runs

Sales operators frequently send `/btw note:` annotations or status commands while an agent is mid-run — checking pipeline status, adding context, or logging observations. Before this fix, those commands would interrupt the active run. Now they're handled as non-blocking annotations that don't disrupt the agent's flow.

### HTTP 421 retries on fallback transport

Network instability — particularly when Telegram's MTProto routes through carrier NAT in certain regions — could cause silent message delivery failures. The fallback transport now automatically retries on HTTP 421 Misdirected Request, improving reliability on flaky mobile networks common in emerging markets.

### Forum-topic origin preserved in delivery

When delivering messages back into Telegram forum threads, the origin topic context is now correctly preserved — preventing replies from landing in the wrong thread in multi-topic group sales rooms.

---

## Expanded Skills Library

OpenClaw's modular skills system gained several new entries:

- **`autoreview`** (renamed from Codex closeout review): Automated quality review of completed agent conversations. For high-volume outreach teams, this lets you run QA at scale — automatically flagging conversations where the agent went off-script, missed a qualification question, or failed to deliver a CTA.
- **meme-maker**: Template search and image rendering. If your playbook includes WhatsApp sticker campaigns or social content, this opens new creative automation options.
- **Python debugging (pdb + remote attach)** and **Node Inspector debugging**: Essential for development teams customizing agent skills or building integrations.
- **Spike workflow**: Lightweight timebox investigation patterns for rapid prototyping.

The `autoreview` skill in particular is worth enabling immediately for any team running high-volume SDR sequences — it replaces manual conversation sampling with automated quality gates.

---

## Browser Automation: Handling Modal Dialogs

If you use OpenClaw's browser automation for lead scraping, LinkedIn prospecting, or form-fill workflows, v2026.5.18 adds proper modal dialog handling:

- Pending and recently handled modals now appear in browser snapshots
- New `blockedByDialog` status surfaces when a modal is blocking automation
- `browser dialog --dialog-id` command to programmatically accept or dismiss pending dialogs

This resolves a common silent failure mode where automated lead-capture flows would stall indefinitely on cookie consent banners, GDPR modals, or login prompts — with no indication that automation had stopped.

---

## Image Processing: Sharp with Fallback Chain

Media handling for image attachments now uses **Sharp** — a high-performance Node.js image processor — with an automatic fallback chain to `sips` (macOS), ImageMagick, or ffmpeg when Sharp isn't available. This improves throughput for workflows processing product catalogs, WhatsApp media attachments, or trade document images at scale.

Additionally, image metadata probing no longer unnecessarily invokes external decoders, reducing subprocess overhead in high-volume media pipelines.

---

## Security Improvements for Enterprise Deployments

Two security changes worth noting if you're deploying in a corporate environment:

**HTTPS managed forward-proxy with scoped CA trust**: If your corporate network routes outbound HTTPS through a managed proxy, OpenClaw v2026.5.18 correctly applies scoped CA trust for that proxy — eliminating SSL certificate errors that were previously common behind enterprise HTTPS inspection proxies.

**Admin HTTP RPC for WhatsApp QR login**: Trusted admin clients can now initiate WhatsApp Web QR login flows via HTTP RPC. This enables headless provisioning of WhatsApp channels without requiring someone to be physically present at the server — a key requirement for cloud-deployed or data-center SDR installations.

---

## Infrastructure Updates

- New `OPENCLAW_IMAGE_APT_PACKAGES` Docker/Podman build argument — inject custom system packages at build time for specialized channel dependencies without forking the base image.
- Pi packages upgraded to 0.75.1; Node.js minimum raised to 22.19.

PulseAgent managed users get these automatically. Self-hosted operators should pull the latest image and rebuild.

---

## What Changed: v2026.5.12 vs v2026.5.18

| Capability | v2026.5.12 | v2026.5.18 |
|---|---|---|
| Android voice | Basic audio path | Realtime Gateway relay |
| Telegram topic routing | Lost on agent handoff | Preserved end-to-end |
| Agent run interruption | `/btw` aborts runs | Non-blocking |
| Browser modal handling | No dialog awareness | Snapshot + programmatic control |
| Image processing | Basic | Sharp + 3-level fallback |
| Enterprise HTTPS proxy | No CA trust | Scoped CA trust |
| WhatsApp provisioning | Requires physical device | Headless via HTTP RPC |
| Skills library | Standard set | +autoreview, meme-maker, debugging |

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Does the Android realtime voice work on older devices?**
The realtime Gateway relay requires Android 9.0+ and a stable connection. WiFi or 4G LTE is recommended. The previous fallback pathway remains available for older devices.

**Will the Telegram topic fix break existing workflows?**
No — it's backward compatible. Workflows not using forum topics are unaffected. Forum-topic-based workflows will now route correctly instead of dropping to the default thread.

**How do I enable the `autoreview` skill?**
Add `autoreview` to your agent's skill list in `agent.yaml`. It runs automatically after each conversation closes and surfaces a structured quality report.

**Is the HTTPS proxy support automatic?**
Set the standard `HTTPS_PROXY` environment variable and OpenClaw v2026.5.18 picks it up, applying scoped CA trust automatically. No extra configuration needed.

**Do self-hosted operators need to rebuild Docker images for the Node.js change?**
Yes — pull the latest base image and rebuild. The Node.js 22.19 minimum is enforced at startup. PulseAgent managed deployments are updated automatically.
